pi_items.source: Web verification - HTTP request to domain returns 200 OK; site confirmed as real SEO testing platform with dedicated hreflang test pages
Domain extraction from target_url; confirmed via HTTP header verification
Matches setup_task.sh line 32 where directory is created; export_result.sh line 14 references same path
Matches setup_task.sh line 33 where directory is created; export_result.sh line 15 references same path

Screaming Frog documentation (screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/tutorials/how-to-audit-hreflang/) confirms hreflang is core validation concept; TechnicalSEO and hreflang.org documentation confirms as primary audit term
Hreflang validation requires language code validation (ISO 639-1); Screaming Frog tutorial discusses language code format validation as critical audit element
Screaming Frog tutorial explicitly states 'reciprocal relationships' and 'return links' as critical hreflang audit concept; all alternate versions must confirm relationship through return links
Hreflang is an HTML tag; core element of any hreflang audit is analyzing the tag itself; multiple sources (TechnicalSEO, Apify) refer to 'hreflang tag' validation

Screaming Frog tutorial identifies common errors including missing reciprocal links, missing self-references, missing language variants; SEO audit standard practice is to report missing implementations
Screaming Frog and hreflang.org documentation discuss validation errors including invalid language codes, HTTP errors, noindex conflicts; error reporting is standard in hreflang audits

Screaming Frog tutorial includes best practices such as including self-referencing hreflang, x-default fallback, ensuring canonical-only references; audit reports conventionally include recommendations for remediation
Design requirement specified in task.json metadata; reasonable minimum for comprehensive audit report covering multiple validation aspects

privileged_info_summary: The task targets https://crawler-test.com/, a real, publicly accessible SEO testing website (HTTP 200 verified) that includes dedicated hreflang test pages at /urls/pages_with_hreflang/ and related paths. Data is sourced from this real website, not synthetic. The required report keywords (hreflang, language, return, tag, missing, error, recommendation) align with standard hreflang validation audit practices documented by Screaming Frog SEO Spider and confirmed by hreflang.org and TechnicalSEO.com. These keywords represent the core elements of hreflang validation: the tag itself, language code validation, reciprocal link verification, identifying missing implementations, detecting errors (invalid formats, HTTP errors, noindex conflicts), and providing recommendations for remediation.
